title: My Anxiety Is Information, Not Identity
subtitle: An affirmation for separating the anxious feeling from the self
excerpt: A short affirmation for the moment anxiety feels like a permanent character trait rather than a passing signal. Separates the experience of anxiety from the self experiencing it.
sourceNotes: Written for homemade.education, drawing on the defusion techniques in acceptance and commitment therapy (ACT) as widely adapted into mainstream self-help practice.

content.text: A short affirmation for when the anxiety has been going on long enough that it starts to feel like a permanent feature of who you are. The affirmation draws a line between the signal and the self.
Say it in the middle of an anxious moment, or in anticipation of one. Three times.
My anxiety is information, not identity.

Information: it is telling you something. That the situation feels unsafe, or uncertain, or too much right now. The information might be accurate or it might be out of proportion. Either way it is a signal, not a definition of you. Sit with the signal. Ask what it is trying to protect. Then decide what to do from there.
Written for homemade.education. The idea of treating emotion as a signal, not as part of who you are, comes from acceptance and commitment therapy (ACT). ACT is a widely used form of CBT.
